concepts.name: System Benchmarking
concepts.description: The evaluation of whether hardware delivers promised performance under realistic ML workloads.

concepts.name: Model Benchmarking
concepts.description: The validation of whether optimization and compression techniques preserve model quality across the full input distribution.

concepts.name: Data Benchmarking
concepts.description: The verification of whether a model generalizes to real-world data with its associated noise, bias, and distributional shift.

concepts.name: Benchmark Gaming
concepts.description: The practice of intentionally optimizing hardware or compilers specifically for a benchmark's characteristics rather than real-world performance.

concepts.name: Energy Benchmarking
concepts.description: The measurement of power consumption and algorithmic energy efficiency in computing systems under ML workloads.

concepts.name: Hardware Lottery
concepts.description: The phenomenon where a model's success is determined by its compatibility with dominant hardware platforms rather than intrinsic algorithmic superiority.

concepts.name: Benchmark Engineering
concepts.description: The practice of fine-tuning hyperparameters or architectures explicitly to maximize evaluation scores rather than deployment performance.

concepts.name: Expected Calibration Error
concepts.description: A metric measuring the gap between a model's predicted confidence scores and its actual accuracy across different confidence bins.

concepts.name: Pareto Frontier
concepts.description: A trade-off curve identifying models that cannot improve one efficiency metric without degrading another.

concepts.name: First-Token Latency
concepts.description: The time required for a language model to generate its first output token, which is dominated by prompt processing.

concepts.name: Benchmark Contamination
concepts.description: A failure mode in large language models where the training corpus includes benchmark test data, inflating evaluation scores through memorization.

concepts.name: Distribution Shift
concepts.description: The phenomenon where the statistical distribution of data encountered in production differs from the training data distribution.

concepts.name: Covariate Shift
concepts.description: A specific type of distribution shift where the input feature distribution changes while the relationship between inputs and outputs remains constant.

concepts.name: Data-Centric AI
concepts.description: A development paradigm that focuses on methodical dataset curation and enhancement to improve system performance rather than changing the model architecture.

concepts.name: Dynamic Benchmarking
concepts.description: An evaluation approach that continuously updates test datasets to prevent saturation as model capabilities advance.

concepts.name: Silent Degradation
concepts.description: A production failure mode where deployed models produce plausible but incorrect outputs without generating explicit error signals.
